diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Cognitive hyperarousal at sleep onset is the core feature of conditioned insomnia.

symptoms.symptom_name: Early-morning awakening (~3 AM) with inability to resettle

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sleep-maintenance and early-waking difficulty are common in psychophysiological insomnia.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Depression / anxiety disorder with sleep disturbance
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Early-morning waking can accompany mood disorder — prompts a mood/SI screen.

symptoms.symptom_name: Sleep-focused anxiety and dread of bedtime (clock-watching)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Conditioned arousal to the bed/bedtime is the defining mechanism of psychophysiological insomnia.
Evening dosing of an activating antidepressant can impair sleep onset — a modifiable contributor for GP medication review.

symptoms.symptom_name: Daytime fatigue with poor concentration and irritability

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Non-restorative sleep produces secondary daytime cognitive and mood impairment.
Daytime cognitive change must be screened for delirium before being attributed to fatigue.

symptoms.symptom_name: Fully oriented, recognises spouse, no fluctuation or hallucinations

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Preserved orientation without fluctuating consciousness or hallucinations makes delirium unlikely.

symptoms.symptom_name: No loud snoring, witnessed apnoeas or waking gasping

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of snoring, witnessed apnoeas and gasping makes OSA an unlikely primary cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: Mild chronic arthritis not the reason for sleeplessness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Patient states pain is not what keeps her awake — pain-driven insomnia is unlikely to be primary.

symptoms.symptom_name: Active suicidal ideation / intent to self-medicate dangerously (gated)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Depression / anxiety disorder with sleep disturbance
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Active suicidal ideation in a distressed, exhausted older adult is a mental-health emergency mandating urgent escalation.
A validated cognitive/delirium instrument cannot be administered to a confused patient over chat — an in-person step the AI cannot perform.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person GP review with medication reconciliation and consideration of CBT-I referral
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The evening-antidepressant contribution and any sedative-hypnotic decision, plus a delirium/mood screen and falls-risk assessment, require an in-person clinician; CBT-I is first-line durable therapy.
Documents sleep architecture and behavioural patterns to guide CBT-I; low-burden and patient-completed.
OSA is unlikely on history (no snoring/apnoeas/gasping); a sleep study is not first-line for a conditioned insomnia and should not be over-ordered.
